skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem: Two box plots labeled Group 1 and Group 2 show the masses (in kilograms) of antelope in each group. The number line ranges from 18 to 30. For Group 1: minimum 19, Q1 = 22, median = 26, Q3 = 28, maximum = 30. For Group 2: minimum 18, Q1 = 23, median = 27, Q3 = 29, maximum = 30. Which of the following statements must be true based on the box plots?

answerOptions.content: The median mass of group 1 is less than the median mass of group 2.
The mean mass of group 1 is less than the mean mass of group 2.
The median mass of group 1 is greater than the median mass of group 2.
The mean mass of group 1 is greater than the mean mass of group 2.

rationale: The median of a box plot is the vertical line inside the box. Group 1's median is 26 kg; group 2's median is 27 kg. Since 26 < 27, the median mass of group 1 is less than the median mass of group 2. The means cannot be determined from a box plot, so the statements about means are not necessarily true.
